column2: Terminates the innermost `for`, `switch`, or `select` statement and transfers execution to the statement immediately following it.
Defines a branch in a `switch` or `select` statement that executes when its condition matches.
Declares a channel type used for communication between goroutines. Channels can send and receive values of a specific type.
Declares constants whose values are determined at compile time and cannot be changed during program execution.
Skips the rest of the current iteration in a `for` loop and proceeds to the next iteration.
Specifies the fallback case in a `switch` statement when no other case matches, or a non-blocking operation in a `select` statement.
Schedules a function call to execute after the surrounding function returns, useful for cleanup operations. Deferred calls execute in LIFO order.
Specifies code to execute when the `if` condition is false.
Forces execution to continue into the next case in a `switch` statement, regardless of whether it matches.
The only looping construct in Go; can be used as a traditional for loop, while loop, or to iterate over ranges.
Declares a function or method with a name, parameters, return type, and body.
Starts a new goroutine (lightweight thread) that runs the specified function concurrently.
Jumps execution to a labeled statement within the same function (rarely used in modern Go code).
Executes code conditionally based on whether a boolean expression is true.
Includes packages in your program, making their exported identifiers available for use.
Declares an interface type that specifies a set of method signatures that a type must implement.
Declares a map type, which is a built-in hash table/dictionary that stores key-value pairs.
Declares the package name at the beginning of every Go source file. Every Go program is made of packages.
Used in `for` loops to iterate over elements of slices, arrays, maps, strings, or channels.
Exits the current function and optionally returns one or more values to the caller.
Waits on multiple channel operations simultaneously, executing the first case that becomes ready (like `switch` but for channels).
Declares a composite data type that groups together variables (fields) under a single name.
Provides multi-way branching based on value comparison or type. More flexible than switch in many other languages.
Declares a new named type or creates a type alias. Used to define structs, interfaces, and custom types.
Declares variables with an explicit type. Variables can be initialized at declaration or take their zero value.
